qa_pairs.question: What is the main purpose of the "Moving between clusters" page on Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: The "Moving between clusters" page explains the similarities and significant distinctions between Alliance clusters, helping users adapt when moving from one cluster to another.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended method for transferring large amounts of data between Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: For transferring data exceeding a few hundred megabytes between Alliance clusters, the use of Globus is recommended.

qa_pairs.question: How do users generally access Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Users generally access Alliance clusters via SSH by changing the cluster name, using the same username and password across all clusters.

qa_pairs.question: Are there any special steps required to access the Niagara cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, accessing Niagara does require a further specific step beyond standard SSH access.

qa_pairs.question: Is data mirrored between the filesystems of different Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, there is no mirroring of data between Alliance clusters; the contents of home, scratch, and project spaces are independent on each cluster.

qa_pairs.question: Can filesystem quota policies differ between Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, filesystem quota policies may differ between Alliance clusters, although generally not by much.

qa_pairs.question: If a research group has a special storage allocation on one Alliance cluster, will it automatically be available on other clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, special storage allocations like `$HOME/projects/rrg-jsmith` are normally only available on the particular Alliance cluster where they were allocated.

qa_pairs.question: Are globally installed software modules generally the same across all general-purpose Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the collection of globally installed modules is the same across all general-purpose Alliance clusters, distributed using CVMFS, assuming the same standard software environment.

qa_pairs.question: What happens to user-installed Python virtual environments or R and Perl packages when moving to a new Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: User-installed Python virtual environments, R packages, or Perl packages will need to be re-installed on the new Alliance cluster using the same steps as on the original cluster.

qa_pairs.question: If a user modifies their `$HOME/.bashrc` file on one Alliance cluster, will these changes automatically apply to another cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: No, if a user modifies their `$HOME/.bashrc` file on one Alliance cluster, they will need to modify the same file on the new cluster they are using.

qa_pairs.question: What system do all Alliance clusters use for job submission?
qa_pairs.answer: All Alliance clusters use Slurm for job submission.

qa_pairs.question: Will job submission scripts generally work across different Alliance clusters without modification?
qa_pairs.answer: Many parts of a job submission script will work across Alliance clusters, but users should check for differences in CPU cores, memory per node/core, and internet access.

qa_pairs.question: What critical cluster-specific details should users verify regarding CPU cores and memory when submitting jobs on Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Users should verify the number of CPU cores per node or per GPU and the amount of memory per node or per core, as these may vary significantly between Alliance clusters.

qa_pairs.question: Do compute nodes on all Alliance clusters have direct Internet access?
qa_pairs.answer: No, on some Alliance clusters, compute nodes may not have direct Internet access, so users should check site-specific policies for the cluster they are using.

qa_pairs.question: Are default research group compute allocations available on every Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, each research group has access to a default allocation (e.g., `#SBATCH --account=def-jsmith`) on every Alliance cluster.

qa_pairs.question: Are special compute allocations like RRG or contributed allocations universally available across all Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, special compute allocations like RRG or contributed allocations are tied to a particular Alliance cluster and will not be available on other clusters.
